scenario: Re-review the revised paper in paper/ — all TBD placeholders are now populated with real data from 220 experiments. Check if the 18 required changes from artifacts/reviews/meta-review.md are addressed. Focus on: real data in tables, statistical limitations acknowledged, regression findings documented, abstract accuracy, conclusion backed by data.

concerns: Experiment was descoped from 9,120-cell matrix (4 models × 3 conditions × 10 reps) to 220 cells (1 model × 2 conditions × 5 reps) — several RC items (RC-4 compound, ablation) are N/A or deferred to future work, which reviewers may not accept
RC-3 (95% CI columns) and RC-18 (semantic macros) are completely unaddressed — tables show raw numbers without \ci{}, \pval{}, \cohend{}, \meanstd{} macros
RC-12 (exact Wilcoxon) is unaddressed — metrics.ts still uses normal approximation with consecutive ranks instead of average ranks, and n dropped from 10 to 5 making the approximation even worse
RC-10 (missing references) and RC-16 (duplicate bib entries) are unaddressed — 4 seminal references missing, carroll2024ai/park2024ai duplicate remains
RC-15 (Perez & Ribeiro attribution) is unaddressed — still says 'formalized by' instead of correct attribution
Page limit (RC-2) status is unclear — still 15 sections with 6 separate results sections, likely exceeding 13-page USENIX limit
feasibility.recommendation: Proceed with the re-review. The paper has made substantial progress (RC-1 data populated, RC-6 statistical columns added, RC-7/RC-14 confounds acknowledged, RC-9 numbers fixed, RC-11 tool-use scoped), but approximately 7-8 of 18 items remain unaddressed. A systematic RC-by-RC verification will produce a clear revision checklist for the final pass.

rationale: This is a research paper re-review task requiring cross-disciplinary verification: data integrity (tables vs. JSON results), statistical methodology (Wilcoxon, CI, power analysis), editorial quality (references, attribution, macros), and holistic assessment (abstract/conclusion accuracy). Four specialist roles mirror the original review panel structure from the meta-review, enabling parallel verification of independent concern areas.

roles.description: Cross-references every number in paper tables (§5-§10, §12) against results/run-2026-04-06T19-30-14/analysis.json. Verifies abstract claims (45.5%, 25.5%, 20pp, specific scenario percentages) match the data. Checks conclusion claims are supported. Confirms no TBD/placeholder remnants exist anywhere in paper/.
roles.why: The core question is whether real data replaced TBD placeholders correctly. Every number in 7 results tables and 1 summary table must match the analysis.json output. A single transcription error undermines the paper's credibility.

roles.description: Evaluates RC-3 (95% CI columns), RC-4 (super-additivity test), RC-5 (power analysis), RC-8 (Bonferroni families), and RC-12 (exact Wilcoxon / tie handling). Reviews src/harness/metrics.ts for implementation correctness. Assesses whether the n=5 design is adequately acknowledged and whether claims are appropriately tempered.
roles.why: Statistical methodology was the most critical concern across all 4 original reviews. The paper claims statistical rigor but 5 of 18 RC items are statistics-related. The Wilcoxon implementation (normal approximation at n=5, consecutive ranks) directly affects reported p-values.

roles.description: Checks RC-2 (page limit), RC-10 (missing references: Wallace 2019, Zeng 2024, Shen 2024, Shafahi 2018), RC-15 (Perez & Ribeiro attribution), RC-16 (duplicate carroll2024ai/park2024ai bib entries), RC-17 (varied section openings), RC-18 (semantic macro usage). Also verifies RC-9 (numerical consistency) and RC-11 (tool-use scoping) are properly addressed.
roles.why: Editorial issues account for 8 of 18 RC items. While individually minor, unaddressed editorial items (wrong attribution, duplicate references, unused macros) signal carelessness to reviewers and can trigger desk rejection at a top venue like USENIX Security.

roles.description: Synthesizes findings from data-validator, statistics-auditor, and editorial-reviewer into a final RC-1 through RC-18 pass/fail/partial assessment. Produces an updated revision-check.md at artifacts/reviews/revision-check.md with evidence-based verdicts. Assesses overall paper readiness for resubmission. Flags any new issues discovered during re-review.
roles.why: The original revision-check.md showed 0/18 pass. This role produces the updated assessment with the current state. The meta-reviewer must also evaluate whether the experiment descoping (9,120→220 cells) is adequately justified and whether deferred items (compound, ablation, multi-model) weaken the paper's claims.

tasks.title: Cross-reference all paper tables against analysis.json
acceptanceCriteria: Every number in tables tab:ci-success, tab:sm-success, tab:cs-success, tab:bc-success, tab:sys-metrics, tab:hitl-success, and tab:summary-all is verified against results/run-2026-04-06T19-30-14/analysis.json with zero discrepancies or all discrepancies documented
Abstract claims (45.5% baseline, 25.5% hardened, 20pp benefit, specific scenario percentages like CI-1=100%, SM-1 100%→0%, BC-2 0%→100%) are verified against data
Conclusion claims (all 5 enumerated findings) are verified as supported by the data in tables
grep for TBD, placeholder, XX.XXXX, example.com across all paper/ files returns zero matches (confirming RC-1 complete)

tasks.title: Verify abstract and conclusion claims match actual experiment scope
acceptanceCriteria: Abstract accurately describes 220-run experiment (22 scenarios × 2 conditions × 5 reps) with single model (GPT-4o-mini), not the original 9,120-cell plan
All five key findings in abstract are verifiable from the data tables (no overclaiming)
Conclusion does not claim results that require multi-model, compound, or ablation data that was not collected
Regression cases (BC-2, CS-2) are accurately described in both abstract and conclusion with correct percentages

tasks.title: Assess RC-3: 95% confidence interval columns in results tables
acceptanceCriteria: Determination of whether any results table includes 95% CI columns (currently none do — verify this is still the case)
Assessment of whether the paper still claims CI reporting in methodology section
If CIs are missing, document the specific tables that need CI columns and recommend Wilson vs. Clopper-Pearson intervals for proportion data
Check if the \ci{}{} macro defined in main.tex is used anywhere in section files

tasks.title: Assess RC-4, RC-5, RC-8, RC-12: statistical framework completeness
acceptanceCriteria: RC-4 assessment: Whether super-additivity test procedure is formally defined in §4.4 or adequately deferred (compound data was not collected — is this acceptable?)
RC-5 assessment: Whether the power limitation paragraph in §4.4 constitutes adequate acknowledgment given n=5 (even less power than originally reviewed n=10)
RC-8 assessment: Whether Bonferroni correction with k=22 is sufficiently defined or whether separate family definitions are still needed
RC-12 assessment: Whether metrics.ts Wilcoxon implementation (normal approximation, consecutive ranks, no continuity correction) is acceptable at n=5 or constitutes a critical flaw

tasks.title: Assess RC-10, RC-15, RC-16: bibliography and attribution issues
acceptanceCriteria: RC-10: Verify whether Wallace et al. 2019, Zeng et al. 2024, Shen et al. 2024, Shafahi et al. 2018 have been added to bibliography.bib and cited in related-work.tex (currently absent)
RC-15: Verify whether related-work.tex:20 still attributes 'formalization' to Perez & Ribeiro (currently still present — document the exact incorrect text)
RC-16: Verify whether carroll2024ai and park2024ai duplicate entries still exist in bibliography.bib (currently still present at lines 433/447)
Document any other orphaned or uncited bibliography entries

tasks.title: Assess RC-2, RC-17, RC-18: structural and formatting issues
acceptanceCriteria: RC-2: Estimate current page count and assess whether the paper fits within 13-page USENIX body limit (6 separate results sections + discussion + related work suggests likely overage)
RC-17: Verify whether results section openings are varied or still follow mechanical pattern (currently 'The N [category] scenarios [verb]...' in all 6 sections)
RC-18: Verify whether semantic macros (\pval{}, \cohend{}, \ci{}{}, \meanstd{}{}, \sig) are used in data tables (currently unused — all tables use raw LaTeX formatting)
Assess overall LaTeX quality and identify any new formatting issues

tasks.title: Assess RC-7, RC-9, RC-11, RC-13, RC-14: content and scoping items
acceptanceCriteria: RC-7: Verify confound acknowledgment in methodology.tex and discussion.tex (currently present as 'Confound note' paragraph — assess adequacy)
RC-9: Verify numerical consistency in related-work.tex (original errors: 2,640 and 600 — now should reference 220 runs matching actual experiment)
RC-11: Verify tool-use attack scoping in discussion.tex limitations (currently item 6 — assess adequacy)
RC-13: Verify whether failure/timeout rates are reported per scenario (currently only mentioned in methodology.tex without a table)
RC-14: Verify seeding clarification in methodology.tex (currently addressed in §4.3)

tasks.title: Produce final RC-1 through RC-18 revision verification
acceptanceCriteria: Updated artifacts/reviews/revision-check.md with pass/fail/partial status for all 18 RC items, with evidence from the three specialist audits
Overall pass rate calculated (estimated ~8-10 of 18 based on preliminary analysis)
Clear prioritized list of remaining items to fix before resubmission, categorized as blocking vs. non-blocking
Assessment of whether the experiment descoping (9,120→220 cells) is adequately justified in the paper and whether it weakens key claims
Final verdict on paper readiness for USENIX Security 2027 resubmission
